item.title: Judge rules Donald Trump's settlement with IRS tried to 'manipulate the judicial process'
item.description: US President Donald Trump's lawsuit against his own government over leaked tax records was an unparalleled exercise in self-dealing, a US judge says in a scathing ruling.

item.title: Jarome Luai joins Parramatta, Benji Marshall slams relationship breakdown rumours
item.description: Benji Marshall has slammed "bull**** rumours" claiming a fractured relationship with Jarome Luai contributed to the star five-eighth's early exit from Wests Tigers.

item.title: Mystery surrounds sudden departure of Mildura Base Public Hospital chief executive
item.description: Mystery surrounds the departure of the chief executive of Mildura Base Public Hospital, who led the north-west Victorian hospital through its transition from private to public.

item.title: Major SA highways given lowest possible safety rating by Australian Road Assessment Program
item.description: Parts of the Sturt Highway, which connects Sydney to Adelaide and provides an integral part of the country's freight network, have been given the lowest possible safety rating by the Australian Road Assessment Program (AusRAP).

item.title: Cyber warning over Russian hackers targeting critical industries
item.description: The Australian Signals Directorate says hackers are trying to access information through network devices, such as laptops, which use old software and predictable passwords.

item.title: Senior minister demands answers from Victoria's infrastructure authority following corruption allegations
item.description: Victorian senior minister Melissa Horne says new allegations about union influence on state construction sites are "alarming", writing to the infrastructure authority to seek assurances they had acted appropriately.

item.title: Racism remains a key issue for AFL players while Indigenous AFLW players rate workplace culture low
item.description: Racism remains a key issue for First Nations AFL players, while Indigenous AFLW players rate their workplace culture significantly lower than their peers, a new report finds.

item.title: Woman strangled by wheelchair lap belt due to staff shortage, court hears
item.description: A woman who died after slipping from her wheelchair and being strangled by its lap belt had been left unsupervised due to a staff shortage at an Adelaide care facility, a court has heard.

item.title: Family of motorcycle crash victim calls for careless driver to face prison time
item.description: The family of a beloved member of the Northern Territory's motorsport community has urged a court to send a woman who failed to give way to him to jail over the fatal crash.

item.title: Giant petrel found on Noosa Main Beach tests negative for H5 bird flu
item.description: The giant petrel was taken to RSPCA Queensland's Wildlife Rehabilitation Centre where it underwent testing by Biosecurity Queensland as part of its surveillance program.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Victorian teachers set to strike again following deadlocked negotiations
item.description: A fresh statewide strike by Victorian public school teachers will he held next week, after negotiations with the Victorian government reached an impasse.

item.title: PBS access to two vital MS treatments under threat
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Launceston General Hospital experiencing 'impacted' capacity due to high demand
item.description: Tasmania's top health official says it will take the Launceston General Hospital "two or three days" to bring its services back to an acceptable level, after it issued a notice urging patients to only attend if it is a medical emergency.

item.title: Angus Taylor rules out One Nation coalition once and for all
item.description: Coalition leader Angus Taylor has renewed his criticism of Pauline Hanson's party and categorically ruled out any alliance with it.

item.title: Anthony Albanese maps out AI future with new national framework
item.description: Labor will develop a new national framework in response to artificial intelligence as it seeks to address the technology’s growing economic and social impacts.

item.title: Donald Trump ditches plan for fee in Strait of Hormuz as Iran and US trade new strikes
item.description: Donald Trump reneges on his pledge to charge a 20 per cent fee on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, a day after saying he would introduce it.

item.title: Spain soars into World Cup final with stunning upset over favourite France
item.description: Spain is through to the World Cup final thanks to a 2-0 win over France.

item.title: Travel quiz, July 15: Dust off your passport and join us on a trip
item.description: Which of these peaks isn't in the Himalayas? And in what country would you find the island of Rapa Nui?

item.title: Art class uses creativity to paint different picture of mental health
item.description: In regional NSW, one program shows that creativity may be the key to better mental health.

item.title: Impressionism's legacy reconsidered in exhibitions at AGSA and Geelong Gallery
item.description: More than 150 years after the first Impressionist exhibition in Paris, the Art Gallery of South Australia and Geelong Gallery cast new light on the influential art movement.

item.title: The Thai child sex exploitation scourge unfolding at bars, restaurants and hotels
item.description: Thailand is struggling against a "hidden" world of child sexual exploitation that requires action from both the tourism industry and law enforcement agencies, according to NGOs.

item.title: Batteries 'cheaper' than gas plants as data centres fuel turbine costs
item.description: Falling battery costs and a data centre-fuelled dash for gas have overturned decades of accepted wisdom: gas is no longer the cheapest way to meet peak power demand.

item.title: Japan ramps up defences amid concerns about China's 'military activities'
item.description: While the world has been watching the rise of China, Japan has been in the middle of its biggest defence shift since World War II.

item.title: Telstra compensation process puts onus on customers, consumer body says
item.description: Telstra is assessing outage compensation claims, but consumer advocates say the process is cumbersome and puts the onus on customers rather than the telco at fault.

item.title: Australia's best-paid executive lives in US and earns 500 times that of average full-time worker
item.description: Australia's most highly paid executive collected a salary nearly 500 times that of the country's full-time average worker.

item.title: What would Australia's economy look like under One Nation?
item.description: From interest rates to policy advice from billionaire buddy Gina Rinehart, here is how One Nation's economic platform could look.

item.title: US, Iran exchange fire as Trump issues new threats — as it happened
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he will not charge a 20 per cent toll on traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, as the US military confirms its blockade of Iran has come into effect.

item.title: Fertiliser company Neutrog fined over smelly manure piles at Kanmantoo
item.description: An Adelaide Hills business that piled chicken manure, coffee grounds and other waste up to twice the allowed height has been fined by a South Australian court after more than 700 complaints about the smell.

item.title: New owner of Rushy Lagoon outlines plans for pine, cattle and tourism
item.description: The buyer of Tasmania's largest farm says in addition to the pine plantation it has planned for the property, it's also considering cropping and leasing out parts of the farm for grazing, tourism and mountain biking.

item.title: 'People want community': Hobby-based clubs tackling loneliness
item.description: Hobby-based pop-up clubs are attracting people from university students and office workers to new migrants and academics.

item.title: Two people killed in truck crash at Heathcote, north of Melbourne
item.description: A car and truck collided about 9.45pm on the McIvor Highway at Heathcote.

item.title: AI-powered glasses improve everyday life for disability sector amid privacy concerns
item.description: For people who are blind, AI-powered smart glasses deliver a level of independence that was once out of reach, but the rise of wearable cameras also raises urgent questions about surveillance, consent and the law.

item.title: Kakadu's closed waterfalls spark stern warning from NT tourism minister
item.description: It is known as the jewel in the Northern Territory's tourism crown, but visitors to Kakadu National Park are being greeted by closure signs blocking roads to key waterfall sites.

item.title: Australia grapples with child psychiatrist shortage as experts warn of consequences
item.description: Experts are warning younger children are presenting with increasingly complex mental health needs as a new report predicts a nationwide shortfall of child and adolescent psychiatrists.

item.title: NRLW players hope NRL broadcast deal brings longer season, full-time salaries
item.description: A $5.3 billion broadcast deal, booming participation and new contracts on the horizon have NRLW stars hopeful their league can soar to new heights.

item.title: ASX rises after US reimposes naval blockade on Iran — as it happened
item.description: The Australian share market gained ground on Wednesday after weaker-than-expected US inflation figures boosted Wall Street and reduced the likelihood of the Federal Reserve lifting interest rates.

item.title: Aerial cull prompts push from sporting shooters to target feral deer in WA national parks
item.description: After a second annual aerial cull sparked controversy in southern WA, hunters say targeting feral deer from the ground is a more humane option.

item.title: Feral deer aerial cull.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Spain's secret to success, Lamine Yamal's streak and Folarin Balogun's confession
item.description: Spain showcases its biggest advantage over France, Lamine Yamal's hot streak goes on and Folarin Balogun opens up on his overturned red card. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: World Cup: France falters at first real hurdle against Spain as superstars fail to shine
item.description: France's 2026 World Cup campaign is ended by a poor 2-0 defeat to Spain, as the much-hyped collection of star forwards fail to turn up for the biggest game yet.

item.title: New political parties are exploiting Victoria's archaic electoral system
item.description: New political parties are seeking to exploit Victoria's anachronistic electoral system in the upper house to harvest voters' preferences and funnel them wherever they please.

item.title: Adelaide Crows player Josh Worrell to face court over Port Road crash
item.description: Adelaide Crows defender Josh Worrell has been reported by police and will face court over a traffic crash last week.

item.title: Scottish traffic cone gets formal Boston welcome after Scots fans' World Cup adventures
item.description: The World Cup's obsession with Scotland fans and traffic cones continues as a witch's hat from Glasgow receives an official welcome from dignitaries in Boston.

item.title: Flights bound for Adelaide cancelled and diverted due to thick fog
item.description: A number of flights have been delayed for take-off at Adelaide Airport due to heavy fog while others were either cancelled or diverted elsewhere.

item.title: US uses explosive one-way drone boats for first time in combat against Iran
item.description: The United States is using one-way drone boats in combat against Iran for the first time in its military history. A defence expert says America is catching on to this "cheaper and more effective" tactic employed by Iran and Ukraine.

item.title: Kangaroo with joey shot with arrow at Lake Wallace
item.description: Community members search for the injured animal, which was spooked off before wildlife volunteers could help.

item.title: Have you heard of vaginismus?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Empty-nesters on how they 'fill the void' left by children
item.description: Experts say there can be unexpected grief for parents that comes from children leaving home.

item.title: Townsville gardener witnesses 'garbage guts' tree frog preying on sunbird
item.description: A Townsville gardener was watering her garden when she witnessed a white-lipped tree frog catch a sunbird mid-flight.

item.title: Timor-Leste scam centre compounds uncovered as police arrest Chinese, Indonesians, Cambodians
item.description: Police in Timor-Leste uncover a major international scam centre in the country, arresting more than 300 Chinese, Indonesian and Cambodian nationals in raids across the capital, Dili.

item.title: Boy, 5, dies after being hit by 4WD in Cloverdale, in Perth's south-east
item.description: A five-year-old boy dies and a 48-year-old woman is critically injured after being struck by a four-wheel drive outside a park in Cloverdale in Perth's south-east,

item.title: The AFL is set on boutique stadiums for AFLW. Players think it is limiting the game
item.description: The AFL has introduced four double-headers this season, meaning games will be played at bigger venues, while the league remains steadfast on boutique grounds for standalone matches.

item.title: Adelaide Parklands works to continue despite parliamentary inquiry
item.description: Acting Premier Kyam Maher says works for the golf course redevelopment and MotoGP will continue despite a petition attracting 13,500 signatures.

item.title: The sledge between Diamonds teammates that helped break the ice after Super Netball Grand Final
item.description: Kate Moloney has had to put her team's Super Netball grand final nightmare aside to focus on the Commonwealth Games. Some gentle ribbing from her Aussie teammate has helped her see the lighter side.

item.title: Disgraced ex-footballer Nicky Winmar removed from AFL Hall of Fame
item.description: Winmar was found guilty earlier this month of three counts of unlawful assault and common law assault.

item.title: Basil Zempilas declares any Labor loss in Secret Harbour by-election 'a win for Liberals'
item.description: WA's Liberal leader says "there is no white flag being waved" in the upcoming Secret Harbour by-election, while indicating any result other than Labor retaining the seat, namely a win by One Nation, will be "a victory for the Liberals".

item.title: More than a quarter of Tasmanians will vote in new electorates, after AEC announces redistribution
item.description: More than a quarter of Tasmanians will vote in new electorates under a long-awaited boundary redistribution announced by the Australian Electoral Commission.

item.title: Tribunal told children had to use toilet in front of others in Cairns police watch house
item.description: Advocates for young people say conditions in a Queensland watch house have not improved since three teenagers' human rights were breached. A tribunal heard the boys had limited privacy and had to use the toilet in front of others.

item.title: Labor aims to shape AI revolution with new data centre and AI copyright rules
item.description: The prime minister unveils Labor's plan for new AI national standards, warning Australia has a narrow window to set AI's "social licence".

item.title: What this World Cup semifinal means to England and Argentina
item.description: It is one of the biggest rivalries in men's World Cup history, and while the teams may downplay the history, England and Argentina fans are ready for another iconic showdown.

item.title: Daylight saving time could become permanent in the US. What's the situation in Australia?
item.description: If the bill becomes law, it will eliminate the need for states to change the clock twice a year.

item.title: 'Critical minerals' bill expands state compulsory acquisition powers
item.description: A private project to build a 1.8 kilometre cable car connecting Airlie Beach to a mountain in a national park could be subject to new laws that critics say will allow for government land grabs.

item.title: Donald Trump's actions in Iran this week show the world he's run out of options
item.description: Donald Trump's actions this week have only confirmed he has run out of options and is now engaged in a simple war of wills, and calculations on who will run out of munitions first, with an enemy that he has underestimated from the start.

item.title: England v Argentina is a story of Maradona's Hand of God, Beckham's kick and Ramsey's 'animals'
item.description: England's World Cup semifinal clash against Argentina is the latest chapter in a sporting rivalry that dates back 60 years to an ill-tempered quarterfinal, featuring cheating, violence and redemption.

item.title: US global trade investigation targets Australian lamb imports
item.description: The United States International Trade Commission is investigating sheep meat imports, including from Australia, as American farmers struggle.

item.title: CDC races to track source of Cyclospora outbreak as cases spike in US
item.description: The US CDC is investigating thousands of possible cases across 34 states, as officials track down the source of the outbreak.

item.title: Trump reverses temporary pause on traffic stops after recent fatal shootings
item.description: US President Donald Trump says federal immigration agents will continue carrying out vehicle stops, reversing a temporary pause announced by officials just a day earlier.

item.title: New Zealand reports first case of H5N1 bird flu detected in brown skua seabird
item.description: The deadly virus was detected in a brown skua seabird near Wellington, although authorities say there is not yet any evidence of mass mortality or impacts on poultry.

item.title: Brisbane Broncos facing near-impossible task to reach NRL finals
item.description: Brisbane must reach 13 wins to have any chance of playing finals in 2026, but even that wasn't enough for them in 2022 and may not be again this year.

item.title: Wine Australia's National Vintage Report 2026 finds lowest harvest since 2000
item.description: Australian wine grape production has fallen to its lowest amount in more than 25 years as the industry adjusts to a combination of depressed market conditions.

item.title: LGBTQIA+ pop-up club operating in former Sydney church ordered to 'cease offensive trading'
item.description: The LGBTQIA+ venue says it is exploring its legal options after being issued with a breach of lease notice just days after opening at a former Sydney church.

item.title: Through my eyes: Daria Saville
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 26 employees sue Meta over claims AI systems selected workers for lay-offs
item.description: Many of the employees in the lawsuit took pregnancy or parental leave, during which time they would not have worked and thus had their measured output reduced.

item.title: Wednesday Finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Medical records and personal details stolen in GP network cyber attack
item.description: Partnered Health, which operates more than 50 general practice and skin cancer clinics across Australia, has identified more than a dozen clinics where it fears patient information was taken.

item.title: Boomanulla Oval in Narrabundah a step closer to Indigenous control
item.description: The transition of ownership is part of a government agreement with the ACT Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Elected Body.

item.title: Oil reserves eased Iran war price shock, but now they are 'spent', IMF says
item.description: The International Monetary Fund says the global economy was spared the worst of the oil price shock because of large reserves. Now they are running out.

item.title: The future of AI
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: US launches new strikes at Iranian military targets in bid to secure Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US Central Command says it launched a 90-minute attack targeting Iran's strategic Greater Tunb Island.

item.title: The Odyssey may be the epic we deserve, but not the one we need
item.description: The scope of Christopher Nolan's latest epic might be awesome but a menagerie of supporting faces and questionable characterisations muddle the director's vision.

item.title: Argentina into World Cup final as England suffers heartbreaking collapse
item.description: Argentina has delivered England its latest World Cup heartbreak, scoring twice at the death to win 2-1 in a gripping semifinal in Atlanta.

item.title: Music quiz: Test your knowledge of instruments, musical terms and classical-inspired pop ballads
item.description: From famous icons and musical terms to unusual instruments and classical influences in popular music, test your knowledge with this quiz.

item.title: I tried to use voodoo to win Race Around The World, and in some ways it worked
item.description: Halfway through the Race Around The World competition, I'm second last on the leaderboard. So, I decide to cheat.

item.title: AI office urged to include artists after Anthony Albanese's copyright promise
item.description: The creative sector hopes there is a space to contribute to the Office of AI, given its content powers a lot of these tools.

item.title: Liberals canvas ideas to address gender, youth and 'deep-rooted' multicultural problem
item.description: Liberal Party HQ has admitted it is "in the political fight of our lives" as it canvasses ideas, including reviving gender quotas, to take the party forward.

item.title: Telstra and telco regulator prepare for grilling over widespread outage
item.description: Telstra prepares for a grilling in parliament about the recent network outage that disrupted Australia, with the communications regulator also set to face tough questions.

item.title: No need to panic about fuel prices as Iran war escalates again, analysts say
item.description: With the price of oil rising and the fuel excise cut due to end, drivers are facing twin threats when it comes to the price of fuel, but experts say Australia is in a much better position compared with when the Middle East conflict began.

item.title: Albanese welcomes AI companies to Australia on our terms. We just don't know what those are yet
item.description: If Australia is truly going to coax major AI companies to choose us rather than the other suitor nations with similar ambitions, some tough decisions need to be made soon.

item.title: Tasmania's electoral boundary shakeup leaves politicians deciding where to run
item.description: Some notable politicians are among the 114,000 Tasmanians displaced by electoral boundary changes, and now have a difficult decision to make — stick with the electorate that voted them in, or move with the communities they've long been aligned with and live in.

item.title: Grant Whelan, victim of violent robbery on Tasmania's east coast, describes attackers as 'pure evil'
item.description: The 76-year-old victim of a violent robbery on Tasmania's east coast has described the people who attacked him as "pure evil".

item.title: Laser technology developed to test alcoholic drinks for methanol
item.description: Adelaide University researchers have developed a way to use lasers to test closed alcohol bottles for methanol, which kills hundreds of people every year.

item.title: Peregrine falcon carer prepares for avian influenza
item.description: Peregrine falcons and wedge-tailed eagles could be among the first native species to contract a strain of bird flu that has recently arrived in Australia.

item.title: Scientists hope bones in underwater 'time capsules' will unlock more prehistory
item.description: Scientists who have been finding fossils in SA's underwater caves are hoping they can give a window into environmental changes.

item.title: Six months after the alleged Invasion Day terror attack, inquiry hears stories of fear
item.description: A day of evidence reflecting on racism, hate and violence directed towards Aboriginal people highlights the ongoing impact of what police described as a potential "mass-casualty event" at a Perth rally.

item.title: Ancestral remains of Rapa Nui people begin long journey home after 143 years at Australian Museum
item.description: The remains of members of the Rapa Nui people from Easter Island are "finally going home" after sitting in the Australian Museum's collection for over a century.

item.title: Man dies and another seriously injured in e-scooter, e-skateboard crash
item.description: Police investigate an e-scooter and electric skateboard crash in Hervey Bay that has left one man dead and one seriously injured.

item.title: ASX flat as SpaceX shares see $1 trillion wiped — as it happened
item.description: The Aussie share market ended the day steady as SpaceX shares see more than $1 trillion wiped from its highs.

item.title: Lionel Messi's World Cup magic inspires Argentina as history beckons in final against Spain
item.description: With England leading 1-0 and Argentina's hopes of back-to-back World Cup titles fading, superstar Lionel Messi once again becomes the hero.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: England boss Thomas Tuchel's tactics allow Lionel Messi to orchestrate Argentina win
item.description: England's World Cup ends in heartbreak again, with questions being asked of its manager's decisions as Lionel Messi plotted Argentina's win and the Three Lions' demise. Here are the quick hits.

item.title: Thomas Tuchel has 'no regrets' as tactics questioned in World Cup capitulation
item.description: Thomas Tuchel defends his tactics after his team became just the second in World Cup history to lose a semifinal after scoring the first goal.

item.title: Argentina players hold up 'Falklands are Argentine' banner after World Cup victory over England
item.description: Argentinian stars hold up a 'The Falklands are Argentine' banner after defeating England at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: UK's Reform proposes round-the-clock protection for MPs after politician's death
item.description: Britain's populist Reform UK, led by Nigel Farage, has called for all lawmakers to be given "full security" if they want it after the alleged murder of Ann Widdecombe, a prominent member of the party.

item.title: League bans Henty footballer Grady Nigsch for 'life', advises opponent to go to police
item.description: A player and assistant coach for a southern NSW AFL team has been handed a "life ban" following a tribunal hearing into eight offences.

item.title: Liberty Bell Bay manganese smelter to close immediately after sale falls through
item.description: The Liberty Bell Bay manganese smelter in northern Tasmania will close immediately after administrators failed to find a buyer for the former GFG Alliance asset.

item.title: Tehran frees US-Iranian woman Dena Karari after almost two years' detention
item.description: Iran has freed a US-Iranian citizen detained since December, 2024 in what Trump says was a 'gesture of goodwill'.

item.title: World Cup final: The story behind Lionel Messi's famous photo with a baby Lamine Yamal
item.description: On Monday morning, Argentinian legend Lionel Messi will go up against rising Spanish superstar Lamine Yamal in the World Cup final, 19 years after their famous photo shoot.

item.title: England experiences more Argentina agony at World Cup, but it won't stop them dreaming
item.description: England aren't going to be bringing the World Cup home, 60 years of hurt due to extend at least another four years after the devastation of another semifinal defeat.

item.title: NT Health boss says Casuarina Plaza mould infestation 'unacceptable'
item.description: A Darwin building with a community health clinic inside has had to close off areas due to a years-long mould infestation across two floors.

item.title: Firmus meets with community over plans to build 'AI factory' at Tailem Bend
item.description: An AI company says its proposed projects have the potential to create hundreds of jobs in South Australia, but residents of one River Murray town say they want to know more about the potential impacts.

item.title: Spotify removes 'AI slop' and seeks transparency from musicians who use AI
item.description: Streaming giant Spotify has stopped short of expunging artificially generated music from its platform, but it is making it easier for listeners to work out that they are listening to tracks produced using AI.

item.title: Gold Coast destroys 30,000 fire ant nests but confidence remains 'pretty low'
item.description: Acting Mayor Mark Hammel says he is "alarmed and pleased" by the number of nests destroyed.

item.title: Former NSW Liberal minister John Sidoti says allegations of misconduct are 'disgraceful'
item.description: Former NSW Liberal government minister John Sidoti says the impacts of allegations of misconduct while in public office have been "disgraceful" and "horrible".

item.title: Spotify is urging artists to share their AI status on its app.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Cyclist bitten by eastern brown snake caught in her bike chain
item.description: The woman, in her 60s, was riding on a northern NSW rail trail when she ran over a two-metre snake that became trapped then bit her.

item.title: Pete Hegseth announces plan to test US troops for low testosterone
item.description: Under the policy US troops will be offered the chance to undergo testosterone replacement therapy.

item.title: Sam Neill died of pneumonia, long-term agent confirms
item.description: New Zealand actor Sam Neill died from pneumonia, his long-time agent Philip Grenz has confirmed.

item.title: Death-defying Argentina continues thrill-seeking act, playing every World Cup game as if it's the last
item.description: Argentina has been the daredevils of this World Cup, and after another incredible comeback to defeat England in the semifinals, its heady mix of emotion and skill, might just deliver back-to-back titles.

item.title: Former prisoners call on Australia to take more forthright approach to foreign detention and 'hostage diplomacy'
item.description: Australians who have endured prolonged detention overseas call on the government to adopt a more strategic approach to cases involving citizens, especially those held by countries that practice "hostage diplomacy".

item.title: Forest Congress calls for end to old-growth logging in Tasmania but environmental groups want more
item.description: A coalition of conservationists, artists and forest industry figures wants an end to old-growth logging in Tasmania but major environmental groups have distanced themselves from the "shared vision statement", saying it does not go far enough.

item.title: Albanese makes AI plan, Liberals ask base for help in 'political fight'
item.description: It might not have been a sitting week but it has been a busy few days in politics with the prime minister's AI announcement, a raft of politicians inking book deals and a spotting of Pauline Hanson and Gina Rinehart in Sicily.

item.title: Hawks Nest petrel tests positive for H5 bird flu in NSW's second confirmed case
item.description: A second petrel found on the NSW Mid North Coast has tested positive for H5 bird flu.

item.title: Sir Keir Starmer addresses last question session as UK leader
item.description: Outgoing UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer, who announced his resignation last month, has taken part in his last weekly grilling from MPs in parliament.

item.title: Truck driver charged with negligent driving over fiery crash at rest stop near Goulburn
item.description: A 28-year-old semi-trailer driver is charged with negligent driving over an explosive collision that saw six trucks destroyed in a fire at a rest stop near Goulburn.

item.title: Pet cat impact on wildlife could be overstated, UQ researchers say
item.description: University of Queensland researchers say the threat posed by pet cats to Australian wildlife could be overstated, but other experts disagree.

item.title: Australia's Hong Kong, Tibetan and Uyghur communities condemn China's new ethnic unity law
item.description: Thirty-nine community groups in Australia and around the world issue a statement rejecting and strongly condemning China's ethnic unity law.

item.title: Drug traffickers jailed over failed plot to smuggle cocaine from PNG by plane
item.description: Two masterminds behind a 2020 plot to bring $80 million worth of cocaine into Australia were sentenced in Victoria’s County Court, five years after a failed drug trafficking flight.

item.title: Henty player given 'life ban' after opposition complaint
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Anthony Albanese defends decision not to attend Garma Festival
item.description: A year ago the Anthony Albanese vowed to attend the Garma Festival every year for as long as he remained prime minister. Now he says he needs a break.

item.title: Wallabies coach Joe Schmidt gambles with selections in Test farewell against Italy in Perth
item.description: Outgoing Wallabies coach Joe Schmidt is rolling the dice in his final Test in charge, relegating one of his key players to the bench for the clash with Italy.

item.title: Closure of manganese smelter Liberty Bell Bay devastates community in northern Tasmania
item.description: Australia's only manganese smelter is ceasing operations, leaving more than 200 employees without work in northern Tasmania.

item.title: Transgender woman's 'humiliating' experience updating details raises discrimination concerns
item.description: What should have been a quick process of updating personal details left Laura Mitchell "insulted" and "humiliated" by the country's largest institutions.

item.title: AI developers to do more to stop growing terrorist, extremist use
item.description: A report finds AI systems can be tricked into coaching extremists how to make bombs and plan attacks.

item.title: Mount Gambier's controversial Cast in Blue celebrates birthday as public art debate continues
item.description: The blue sculpture that's been the subject of love, loathing, debate and international headlines for googly eyed-vandalism has made it to its first birthday relatively unscathed.

item.title: Aboriginal man, 21, dies at Clarence Correctional Centre in Grafton
item.description: A 21-year-old Aboriginal man has died at the privately-run Clarence Correctional Centre in northern NSW.

item.title: Only one third of ambulances respond on time for priority 3 patients in South Australia
item.description: Data obtained by the South Australian opposition shows fewer ambulances are arriving on time to priority 3 and 4 patients.

item.title: 'Walsh was unbelievable': Broncos keep season alive with upset win
item.description: Brisbane fullback Reece Walsh has been described as "unbelievable" by Penrith's coach after leading the Broncos to an upset win on Thursday, ending an eight-match losing streak and keeping their slim finals chances alive.

item.title: Member for Barker and staffer injured in 'man overboard' incident after oyster farm tour
item.description: Tasmanian police say a man fell from an oyster barge when it hit a sandbar and was briefly trapped. Member for Barker Tony Pasin says he and a staffer were injured and now receiving medical treatment, but it is not clear who fell from the barge.

item.title: Thursday finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: 'I covered my ears': Scott responds as Cats injury crisis worsens
item.description: Geelong beats St Kilda at Kardinia Park to kick off round 19 of the AFL, but the injury list gets longer with a concerning leg issue for Max Holmes.

item.title: New monkey discovered with orange lips in Democratic Republic of Congo
item.description: The monkey — colobus congoensis — is only the fifth new species of African monkey to be discovered in the past 75 years.

item.title: Woodside takes climate activists to Supreme Court over Perth HQ stink-bomb protest
item.description: Energy giant Woodside has taken climate activists to court in a bid to unmask the conspirators behind a stink-bomb protest that forced the evacuation of its Perth headquarters.

item.title: SA Police arrest alleged 'fight club' organiser, seven other teenagers reported
item.description: SA Police have arrested the alleged organiser of a 'fight club' and reported seven other teenagers they allege were linked to videos of amateur bouts that were posted online.

item.title: NT government slammed for federal racism inquiry no-show as committee hears evidence in Darwin
item.description: A national inquiry into racism against First Nations people, launched after an alleged terror attack in Perth, has heard allegations of systemic racism in the Northern Territory, as the territory government comes under fire for snubbing the probe.

item.title: Home ownership rates in some states are improving, Sydney is 'going backwards'
item.description: Sydney is no longer affordable for most, as new data reveals the number of people who own the home they live in is the lowest it has been in decades.

item.title: NZ's South Island struck by magnitude-5.9 earthquake
item.description: The earthquake struck 40 kilometres north of Te Anau, with the west coast of the South Island from Milford Sound to Puysegur Point, likely to be affected.

item.title: Who is in charge of the Strait of Hormuz?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Officer injured as disturbance unfolds at Perth's maximum-security Casuarina Prison
item.description: A prison officer has been injured in a major disturbance that caused significant damage at Perth’s maximum-security prison.

item.title: Multiple sclerosis patients given PBS lifeline amid drug pricing dispute
item.description: Health Minister Mark Butler has accepted independent advice for a rapid review of MS drugs to ensure they remain available on the PBS.

item.title: Charges expected to be laid over Laos methanol poisoning deaths, ABC understands
item.description: A Melbourne father whose daughter died from methanol poisoning in Laos has said he is "disgusted" over the inadequacy of charges expected to be laid.

item.title: Zelenskyy's dismissal of Ukrainian defence minister sparks public row, protests
item.description: A dispute between the now former defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov and Kyiv's top general is spilling into public, after Volodymyr Zelenskyy orders a second reshuffle of this year.

item.title: Mike Connor visited the same park for 40 years. His notebooks filled with birds are invaluable
item.description: Mike Connor visited the same park for 40 years and wrote down what he saw. He didn't realise he was incidentally chronicling a changing environment and contributing to science.

item.title: In Tokyo, a manga by a convicted cannibal made me question everything
item.description: In a rare bookstore in Japan, I find a manga written by a convicted cannibal. It becomes the subject of a moral dilemma I've been grappling with as a filmmaker.

item.title: ABC news quiz: The iconic film Sam Neill rejected, England's World Cup misery, a royal reunion and a mystery outbreak
item.description: Our weekly quiz has the film Sam Neill "didn't think was funny", plus the sweetness at the centre of our galaxy.

item.title: Dr Morag MacSween is calling on the court to improve its understanding of perpetrator tactics.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Alleged DV victim labelled 'difficult witness', family court permanently removes children
item.description: The Family Court granted John full custody of his kids, but he returned them to their mother, saying the judge got it wrong.

item.title: These tankers chose a Strait of Hormuz route, triggered Iran and lit up the Gulf
item.description: The commercial tankers struck as they transited the Strait of Hormuz in recent weeks were not random, but part of a "highly, highly targeted" campaign run by Tehran, according to marine traffic watchers.

item.title: Workers' rights to drive AI debate at Labor conference, insiders say
item.description: Labor insiders expect ongoing debate about artificial intelligence when party faithful gather to hash out a policy platform to guide the next three years.

item.title: The zeitgeist doesn't suit Angus Taylor but he could do more to help himself
item.description: The mismatch between where the public resides and what the Liberals are expounding is a deeper challenge — a gulf perhaps unable to be bridged, at least in the short term.

item.title: Vaping rates among younger Australians plunge as smoking falls to historic low
item.description: Vaping and smoking rates are on the decline, but a shift to other nicotine products could represent an increase in average use.

item.title: Trump teleprompter operator suspended over bets on president's speeches
item.description: US President Donald Trump's longtime technician allegedly made over $142,000 by placing bets on what Donald Trump would say in speeches.

item.title: Amid the rise of fringe politics, Jacinda Ardern champions empathetic leadership
item.description: The former New Zealand prime minister reflects on leading through crisis, becoming a mother while running a country and why she believes voters are craving something different.

item.title: South Korea wants to be a top-three AI superpower. Here's why memory chips are key
item.description: South Korea is doubling down on its bid to lead in AI, unveiling plans last month to spend hundreds of billions on developing its chip manufacturing.

item.title: Former diplomat says Telstra 'priority' service failures putting wife's life at risk
item.description: Telstra promised Alastair Gaisford that his wife's healthcare was a priority. But he says 15 landline outages, including some that could have prevented the ability to call Triple Zero in an emergency, have convinced him otherwise.

item.title: Future of Liberty Bell Bay site in doubt following manganese smelter's closure
item.description: Australia's only manganese alloy smelter has closed, leaving the future of the site uncertain and the country more reliant on imported alloys for steel production.

item.title: Dealers warn EV drivers of repair and refund delays under warranty from new brands
item.description: As an influx of car brands enters the market, dealers ask for a change to consumer laws to fix a grey area that makes them responsible for warranty repairs.

item.title: Gusty winds to hit south-east Queensland as far north braces for cold blast
item.description: A combination of two strong weather systems will bring wind and rain to south-east Queensland, while residents further north could see the mercury dip to single digits.

item.title: Youth justice 'circuit breaker' draws parallels to Newman-era boot camps
item.description: An alternative sentencing option put forward by the Queensland government is being compared to controversial boot camps of yesteryear.

item.title: Households evicted from public housing triples under Queensland's new behaviour policy
item.description: Under the new policy, tenants who receive three warnings for serious behaviour, including aggressive language, harassment and deliberate damage, in a year are evicted.

item.title: Australian Paige Raddatz racing in Japan after four years away
item.description: Paige Raddatz's road to racing at the famed Fuji Speedway against quality drivers had speed bumps along the way.

item.title: Opals master coach Sandy Brondello turns to 'different style' in bid to match USA dominance
item.description: No country has been able to topple the United States at either the FIBA World Cup or the Olympics since 2006. Here's what Sandy Brondello is doing to try to get Australia's Opals over the hump.

item.title: What is pneumonia? The common infection that caused Sam Neill's death
item.description: After the actor's death from pneumonia, here's what to know about the common infection, its symptoms and why it can sometimes become deadly.

item.title: Natasha never expected to become addicted to prescribed opioids while battling endometriosis
item.description: Natasha Trajkovski never imagined her prescribed opioid medication to help with the pain of endometriosis would lead her to addiction.

item.title: ATO questioned over $73m move into controversial Adelaide office tower
item.description: The Australian Taxation Office will spend at least $73.4 million to relocate its offices into "the best building in Adelaide", four streets away.

item.title: Horsham council rejects mining company WIM Resource's invitation to join reference group
item.description: A regional council has declined a mining company's invitation to join a community reference group and slammed the company for its attitude towards community concerns.

item.title: RSV vaccination rates halve NSW hospitalisations, as influenza peak looms
item.description: New South Wales health authorities have hailed a vaccination program that has nearly halved hospitalisations for a potentially deadly respiratory virus among newborn babies a "fantastic" success, as the winter illness peak fast approaches.

item.title: Driver flips car during police chase in Melbourne's north-east
item.description: Police arrest a driver and his passenger after a high-speed chase through Melbourne's north-eastern suburbs.

item.title: Tim Merlier claims stage 12 of Tour de France as Fernando Gaviria fractures collarbone
item.description: A rider suffers a fractured collarbone in a chaotic conclusion to stage 12 of the Tour de France.

item.title: Live: 'Great mates': Joyce shuts down talk of rift with Hanson after far-right podcast
item.description: Barnaby Joyce has defended his relationship with One Nation leader Pauline Hanson after she sat down for a podcast conversation with UK far-right activist Tommy Robinson. Follow live.

item.title: Apollo boilerplate to be centrepiece of Powerhouse Parramatta's opening exhibition
item.description: It took 43 days and a custom cage to bring the 4.5-tonne test command module from Washington to Castle Hill in Sydney's north-west.

item.title: Ultra-low tides bring rare chance to walk to an island lighthouse
item.description: A rare chance to walk 1 kilometre offshore to an island lighthouse attracts thousands of trekkers to a North Queensland town.

item.title: Live: Telstra CEO to be grilled over nationwide outage and compensation
item.description: Telstra CEO Vicki Brady is set to be grilled over the nationwide outage, and potential compensation. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Opponents of NT child protection bill show 'moral bankruptcy', CLP's Clinton Howe says
item.description: Fierce debate has surrounded the Northern Territory government's child protection reforms — which will return to parliament for debate next week — with one MLA saying those opposing it have shown "a degree of moral bankruptcy".

item.title: Uber removes 'unacceptable' surcharge for Assist users after outcry
item.description: Uber has reversed its decision to impose a $5 fee on passengers requiring extra assistance, after advocates and service providers labelled it discriminatory and a "disability tax".

item.title: Australian golfers struggle at The Open as American Jackson Suber leads
item.description: Australia's golfers struggle again at The Open as an unlikely American holds the lead.

item.title: Lack of housing in remote WA town endangering school's future
item.description: A remote school 500km south-east of Perth is struggling to retain staff as housing pressures force incoming teachers to live far from work.

item.title: Groundwater concerns grow as state pushes ahead with Taroom Trough oil plans
item.description: An expert is warning that the push to fast-track approvals for the proposed Taroom Trough lacks crucial detail.

item.title: Revered surfboard designer and intersex advocate Kyra Jonsun dies in fire
item.description: Kyra Jonsun is being remembered as an advocate for gender-diverse people and a revered surfboard designer following her death in Wollongong last weekend.

item.title: Victorian government tries to head off teacher strike with new offer
item.description: The government says its new offer amounts to a 28.3 per cent pay increase for teachers, principals and support staff over the next four years.

item.title: Ngura Kutju set up in Alice Springs to advocate for Indigenous families in NPY Lands
item.description: A new hub to support families caught between three child protection agencies has been established in Alice Springs in a 12-month trial.

item.title: Three men arrested over Coles Bay robbery and arson in which victim had to watch home burn down
item.description: Three men are arrested in separate Launceston suburbs over a violent armed robbery and house fire at Coles Bay.

item.title: Pauline Hanson blames end of White Australia policy for migration issues on Tommy Robinson's podcast
item.description: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson has blamed the end of the White Australia policy for migration issues in the country during a controversial podcast appearance with a far-right activist in the UK.

item.title: Canberra United FC has a buyer, ending years of uncertainty for the capital's A-League Women's team
item.description: The new owners of the national capital's A-League Women side have pointed to the launch of a men's team.

item.title: When is the World Cup final? How to watch Spain vs Argentina
item.description: The World Cup final is nearly upon us, with Spain to face Argentina for the ultimate prize. Here's everything you need to know about the biggest game in football.

item.title: Spain trains in 'hazardous' air ahead of World Cup final with Argentina
item.description: World Cup finalists Spain train in less-than-ideal conditions in New Jersey as wildfires cause excessive smoke in the north-east of the United States.

item.title: Central Queensland residents warn of fire risks on overgrown crown land
item.description: A central Queensland man is concerned his neighbour's overgrown plot is a fire hazard. His neighbour is the state government.

item.title: Hume Highway partially closed at Breadalbane after fiery truck crash
item.description: A truck has burst into flames after a crash involving another heavy vehicle on the Hume Highway south-west of Goulburn.

item.title: Live: Trump prime-time address expected to cover election denial claims
item.description: US President Donald Trump is set to give a prime-time national address about newly declassified intelligence on investigations into elections. Follow live.

item.title: Donald Trump to deliver nationwide address on 'free and fair elections'
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Destructive cyclone season has communities in northern WA still cleaning up
item.description: Two cyclones in less than a month caused significant damage to the Dampier Peninsula in the Kimberley region during the 2025-26 wet season. Months later, residents say costly repairs are continuing.
